summary.brief: Connette v. Charlotte-Mecklenburg Hospital Authority. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: On 11 September 2010, an emergency room visit for an upper respiratory infection revealed that three-year-old Amaya Gullatte was tachycardic
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The cardiologist recommended the performance of an 'ablation procedure' on Amaya’s heart
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: anesthetics team consisting of anesthesiologist James M. Doyle, M.D. and Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist (CRNA) Gus C. VanSoestbergen utilized a mask to administer the anesthetic sevoflurane
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Shortly after she was induced with the sevoflurane, Amaya went into cardiac arrest.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: the approximately thirteen minutes of oxygen deprivation which was experienced by the child resulted in the onset of permanent brain damage, cerebral palsy, and profound developmental delay.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiffs were prepared to present evidence through certified registered nurse anesthetist Dean Cary acting as an expert witness
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: the trial court’s exclusion of plaintiffs’ expert testimony regarding the allegedly negligent anesthesia plan as a claimed breach of the applicable professional standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We determine that it is timely and appropriate to overrule Byrd as it is applied to the facts of this case.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, we reverse and remand this matter to the trial court for further proceedings consistent with this opinion.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with symptoms of an upper respiratory infection and was found to be tachycardic, leading to a referral to a cardiologist.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Tachycardia in the context of upper respiratory infection

requestables.name: Cardiology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with cardiologist regarding tachycardia and cardiomyopathy

reveal.clinical_significance: Identified underlying heart condition requiring intervention
reveal.decision_impact: Led to decision for ablation procedure

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with upper respiratory infection and tachycardia

timeline_phases.name: Ablation Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes anesthesia induction for ablation procedure

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient undergoing anesthesia induction for ablation procedure
decision_points.prompt: What method of anesthesia induction should be used?

options.clinical_reasoning: Safer alternative for patients with cardiomyopathy
Chosen by anesthesiology team but not ideal for cardiomyopathy

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant and anesthesiologist agreed on this method

expected_action_court.description: Intravenous induction with etomidate should have been used
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was the safer method

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Sevoflurane mask induction was not appropriate for a patient with cardiomyopathy
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Intravenous induction with etomidate should have been used
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered oxygen deprivation leading to cerebral palsy and global developmental delay

key_findings: CRNA not liable as the physician was responsible for the anesthesia plan
Court upheld that the physician bears sole responsibility for treatment decisions

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Alternative anesthetic method could have prevented cardiac arrest

factual_timeline.event: Patient presents with upper respiratory infection and tachycardia
Patient undergoes ablation procedure with sevoflurane mask induction
Emergency room visit for upper respiratory infection

diagnoses.diagnosis: Cerebral palsy and global developmental delay

complications.complication: Oxygen deprivation leading to cerebral palsy

court_findings.finding: No liability found for CRNA VanSoestbergen
CRNA not liable as physician responsible for anesthesia plan
court_findings.legal_significance: Physician bears sole responsibility for treatment decisions
